detail: Guard's Engine seam fires only on rule fall-through, never second-guessing an explicit deny/ask match -- deterministic safety rules stay non-negotiable. Fallback on an unparseable/empty engine reply is ASK (fail-safe), not the permissive default, since a caller who explicitly wired a real engine presumably wants some judgment rather than silent pass-through; the no-engine-configured case (Guard's own default) is untouched and still returns self.default, so existing callers see zero behavior change

added Guard(engine=...) judgment seam: on fall-through, asks the Engine to classify an unmatched Action as ALLOW/ASK/DENY in one word. Live-verified against the real claude CLI: curl-pipe-to-bash -> DENY, ls -la -> ALLOW, npm install left-pad -> ASK. 17 tests green (5 new), ruff clean
